drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local rituximab administration guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway). An additional dose of rituximab 375mg/m² was given on day -2, cycle 1 only in the original CORAL study (not in Aria but can be added manually).
Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (20mg/ml).
AUC 5 (max 790mg set in Aria to comply with national dose bands; maximum dose 800mg). Intravenous infusion in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml).

Pre-ifosfamide uroprotection. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 100ml over 15 minutes. Dose banded per national dose bands (100 NS). | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

Total daily dose 5000mg/m² given as two infusions of 2500mg/m² each over 12 hours (run one after the other consecutively). Mixed in the same bag as mesna (2500mg/m²). Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 12 hours. Dose banded per national dose bands (80mg/ml).

Mixed in same bag as ifosfamide (2500mg/m² x2 bags over 12 hours each). Total 5000mg/m² per day. Dose banded per national dose bands. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

Post-ifosfamide uroprotection. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 8 hours. To start immediately at end of last ifosfamide/mesna infusion bag. May alternatively be given as oral mesna 1800mg/m² (rounded to nearest 400mg) at 0, 2 and 6 hours after end of last ifosfamide infusion. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

dose_modifications.condition: Etoposide: hepatic impairment Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Etoposide: hepatic impairment Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units/L
Etoposide: significant reduction in albumin levels
Ifosfamide: hepatic impairment Bilirubin >20 µmol/L or AST/ALT >2.5xULN or ALP >2.5xULN

dose_modifications.notes: May reduce dose for next cycle

dose_modifications.condition: Ifosfamide: NCI-CTC grade 2 or worsening neurological toxicity despite dose reduction

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L and unsupported platelets ≥50x10⁹/L. No dose modifications for haematological toxicity.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 min before rituximab)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous (30 min before rituximab)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min before rituximab)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day for 5 days oral or intravenous (starting 15-30 min before chemo)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral or intravenous
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day for 5 days oral or intravenous
Allopurinol 300mg once a day oral (cycle 1 only)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once a day on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral
special_warnings: Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: methylene blue 50mg four times a day intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes can be used to prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy. Risk factors: low albumin, renal impairment, prior cisplatin, poor performance status, CNS tumour, bulky pelvic disease, concomitant psychotropic drugs, younger age.
Urine diptest for protein every 4 hours the day of and day after ifosfamide administration. Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours. Urine output must be maintained above 100ml/hour.
Rituximab: cytokine release syndrome risk (usually within 1-2 hours of first infusion). Medicinal products for allergic reactions must be available. Monitor for PML.
Carboplatin: AUC-based dosing - creatinine clearance must be measured (EDTA or calculated) prior to each cycle.
Growth factors (G-CSF) to be continued until neutrophil count above 1x10⁹/L: filgrastim/bioequivalent 30 million units SC from day 6, or pegfilgrastim 6mg SC day 4.
Check hepatitis B status before starting rituximab.
In female patients consider norethisterone 5mg three times a day oral to delay menstruation.
Ifosfamide and mesna infusions should be run one after the other consecutively (not simultaneously). Total ifosfamide dose over 24 hours is 5000mg/m² in 2000ml sodium chloride 0.9%.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg. Two additional doses of rituximab on days 21 and 42 after end of last R-IVAC to total 8 doses.

2000mg/m² every 12 hours on days 1 and 2 (4 doses total). Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. Doses given at 12-hour intervals. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (100mg/ml). For patients aged 65 years and below (higher dose than RIVAC-66).
Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (20mg/ml).

Pre-ifosfamide uroprotection bolus. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 100ml over 15 minutes. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (100mg/ml). | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.
Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 60 minutes. Mixed in the same bag as mesna (1500mg/m²). Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (80mg/ml).

Mixed in same bag as ifosfamide (1500mg/m²). Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 60 minutes. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

Post-ifosfamide uroprotection. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 12 hours. Starting immediately after the ifosfamide/mesna combination bag. On day 5 may be replaced with oral mesna 360mg/m² (rounded to nearest 400mg capsule) at 0, 2 and 6 hours post ifosfamide infusion. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.
Intrathecal administration (not IV). Given intrathecally on day 5. For patients with CNS disease at diagnosis (first cycle of R-IVAC only): also give cytarabine 70mg intrathecally on days 7 and 9.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50%. Escalate doses on subsequent cycles in absence of toxicity.

dose_modifications.condition: Etoposide: hepatic impairment Bilirubin 30-51 µmol/L or AST/ALT 60-180 units/L
Etoposide: hepatic impairment Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST/ALT >180 units/L
Etoposide: significant reduction in albumin levels
Ifosfamide: hepatic impairment Bilirubin >20 µmol/L or AST/ALT >2.5xULN or ALP >2.5xULN

dose_modifications.notes: May reduce dose for next cycle

dose_modifications.condition: Ifosfamide: NCI-CTC grade 2 neurological toxicity or worsening despite dose reduction

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils ≥1x10⁹/L and unsupported platelets ≥75x10⁹/L. No dose modifications for haematological toxicity.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous bolus (30 min before rituximab)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous bolus (30 min before rituximab; may omit if patient already on corticosteroids)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min before rituximab)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day for 7 days oral or intravenous bolus (starting 15-30 min before chemo)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day for 7 days then when required oral or intravenous bolus
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day for 7 days oral or intravenous bolus
Prednisolone 0.5% or dexamethasone 0.1% eye drops one drop into both eyes four times a day for 4 days (cytarabine conjunctivitis prophylaxis)
Folinic acid 15mg four times a day for one day starting 24 hours after intrathecal methotrexate
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral
Pentamidine 300mg nebule once a month
Fluconazole 50mg once a day oral
special_warnings: Multiple protocol versions in use: RIVAC-65 is for patients aged 65 years and below; RIVAC-66 is for patients aged 66 and above or poor performance status. Ensure correct version is used.
Alternating with R-CODOX-M: order R-CODOX-M, R-IVAC, R-CODOX-M, R-IVAC. Two additional rituximab doses on days 21 and 42 after last R-IVAC to total 8 rituximab doses.
Cytarabine TWICE a day warning: cytarabine doses given at 12-hour intervals on days 1 and 2.

Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: methylene blue 50mg four times a day IV infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% can be used to prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy. Risk factors include low albumin, renal impairment, prior cisplatin, poor performance status, CNS tumour.
Ifosfamide and mesna mixed in same bag: combination bag given over 60 minutes, followed by mesna 900mg/m² over 12 hours.

Intrathecal chemotherapy: Methotrexate 12.5mg intrathecal on day 5 (national guidance HSC 2008/001 must be followed). For CNS disease: additional cytarabine 70mg intrathecal on days 7 and 9 (cycle 1 only).
Corticosteroid eye drops required for cytarabine-associated conjunctivitis prophylaxis.
Urine dip test for proteinuria/haematuria every 4 hours day of and day after ifosfamide. Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours. Urine output above 100ml/hour.
Check hepatitis B status before rituximab. Rituximab: monitor for PML.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg. Two additional doses of rituximab on days 21 and 42 after end of last R-IVAC to total 8 doses.

1000mg/m² every 12 hours on days 1 and 2 (4 doses total). Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. Doses given at 12-hour intervals. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (100mg/ml). For patients aged 66 and above or poor performance status (lower dose than RIVAC-65).
Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (20mg/ml).

Pre-ifosfamide uroprotection bolus. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 100ml over 15 minutes. Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (100mg/ml). | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 60 minutes. Mixed in the same bag as mesna (1000mg/m²). Dose banded per nationally agreed bands (80mg/ml). Reduced dose compared to RIVAC-65 due to older age/poor performance status.

Mixed in same bag as ifosfamide (1000mg/m²). Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 60 minutes. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

Post-ifosfamide uroprotection. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 12 hours. Starting immediately after the ifosfamide/mesna combination bag. On day 5 may be replaced with oral mesna 360mg/m² (rounded to nearest 400mg capsule) at 0, 2 and 6 hours post ifosfamide infusion. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.
Intrathecal administration (not IV). Given intrathecally on day 5. For patients with CNS disease at diagnosis (first cycle of R-IVAC only): also give cytarabine 70mg intrathecally on days 7 and 9.

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 50%. Escalate doses on subsequent cycles in absence of toxicity.

Alternating with R-CODOX-M: order R-CODOX-M, R-IVAC, R-CODOX-M, R-IVAC. Two additional rituximab doses on days 21 and 42 after last R-IVAC to total 8 rituximab doses.
Cytarabine TWICE a day warning: cytarabine doses given at 12-hour intervals on days 1 and 2.
Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: methylene blue 50mg four times a day IV infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% can be used to prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy.
Ifosfamide and mesna mixed in same bag: combination bag given over 60 minutes, followed by mesna 600mg/m² over 12 hours.

Intrathecal chemotherapy: Methotrexate 12.5mg intrathecal on day 5 (national guidance HSC 2008/001 must be followed). For CNS disease: additional cytarabine 70mg intrathecal on days 7 and 9 (cycle 1 only).
Corticosteroid eye drops required for cytarabine-associated conjunctivitis prophylaxis.
Urine dip test for proteinuria/haematuria every 4 hours day of and day after ifosfamide. Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours. Urine output above 100ml/hour.
Check hepatitis B status before rituximab. Rituximab: monitor for PML.

drugs.notes: Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% as per local guidelines. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg.

Intravenous injection over 10 minutes. Vesicant. Maximum lifetime cumulative dose of epirubicin is 900mg/m². Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Discontinue epirubicin if cardiac failure develops. If dose reductions made at cycle 1 and well tolerated, dose increases may be considered on subsequent cycles.
Intravenous infusion in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands.

Pre-ifosfamide uroprotection. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 100ml over 15 minutes. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.
1500mg/m² twice a day (total daily dose 3000mg/m²). Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 12 hours. Mixed in the same bag as mesna (1500mg/m²). Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands.

Mixed in same bag as ifosfamide (1500mg/m² twice a day). Total daily dose 3000mg/m². | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

Post-ifosfamide uroprotection. Intravenous infusion in sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml over 12 hours. To start immediately after the final ifosfamide/mesna infusion bag. | Mesna given as multiple fractions — dose represents one administration. Total daily Mesna dose = sum of all Mesna entries.

dose_modifications.condition: Epirubicin: hepatic impairment Bilirubin 30-50 µmol/L or AST/ALT 2-4xULN
Epirubicin: hepatic impairment Bilirubin 51-85 µmol/L or AST/ALT >4xULN
Epirubicin: CrCl less than 10 ml/min (severe renal failure)
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction in severe renal failure

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 min before rituximab)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous (30 min before rituximab; may omit if patient already on corticosteroids)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min before rituximab)

Ondansetron 8mg twice a day for 5 days oral or intravenous
Allopurinol 300mg once a day oral (cycle 1 only)
Aciclovir 400mg twice a day oral
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once a day on Monday, Wednesday and Friday oral
special_warnings: Epirubicin: maximum lifetime cumulative dose is 900mg/m². Baseline LVEF should be measured in patients with history of cardiac problems, cardiac risk factors, or elderly. Discontinue epirubicin if cardiac failure develops.
Epirubicin is a VESICANT - extravasation precautions required.
Ifosfamide encephalopathy risk: methylene blue 50mg four times a day IV infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% can be used to prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy.
Urine dip test for protein every 4 hours the day of and day after ifosfamide. Fluid balance monitoring every 4 hours. Urine output above 100ml/hour.
Rituximab: cytokine release syndrome risk. Monitor for PML. Check hepatitis B status before starting.
Growth factors (G-CSF) to be continued until neutrophil count above 1x10⁹/L: filgrastim from day 7 or pegfilgrastim day 5.
In female patients consider norethisterone 5mg three times a day oral to delay menstruation.
Ensure adequate cardiac function before starting therapy. EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle.

drugs.notes: 20mg once a day days 1-21 inclusive of 28-day cycle. Available as 20mg, 15mg, 10mg, 7.5mg, 5mg and 2.5mg capsules. Swallow whole with water. Pregnancy prevention programme mandatory.

Cycle 1: rituximab 375mg/m² IV on days 1, 8, 15, 22. Cycles 2-5: rituximab 375mg/m² IV on day 1 only. Cycles 6-12: lenalidomide only (no rituximab). Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway).

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery. Restart at full dose.
Start at 10mg once a day. Can increase to 15mg after 2 cycles if no response.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: CrCl less than 30 ml/min (not requiring dialysis)
Lenalidomide: CrCl less than 30 ml/min requiring dialysis
dose_modifications.notes: 5mg once a day. On dialysis days, administer following dialysis.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: non-haematological NCI-CTC grade 3 or above toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below. Reduce to 15mg, then 10mg, then 5mg on subsequent occurrences. Stop if 5mg not tolerated.
Interrupt lenalidomide. Restart at next lower dose when TLS resolves to grade 0.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: venous thrombosis or embolism NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment and start anticoagulation. May be reinstated at clinician's discretion.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 min before rituximab IV)
Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous (30 min before rituximab IV)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min before rituximab IV)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral
Allopurinol 300mg once a day for 7 days cycle 1 only (TLS prophylaxis in patients at risk)
Thromboprophylaxis for patients with additional VTE risk factors
special_warnings: Multiple protocol versions in use - ensure correct protocol for relevant diagnosis.
Pregnancy prevention programme: patient, prescriber and pharmacy must comply. Lenalidomide is teratogenic.
DVT prophylaxis mandatory for patients with additional VTE risk factors. VTE risk assessment prior to starting treatment.
Tumour lysis syndrome: weekly biochemistry monitoring during cycle 1. Adequate hydration required. TLS prophylaxis (allopurinol/rasburicase) prior to cycle 1.
Formal medical review at least by end of first 8 weeks of treatment to determine whether to continue.
If rituximab discontinued for toxicity, lenalidomide may continue up to maximum 12 cycles.
Thyroid function tests at baseline and regular intervals. Calcium and magnesium levels monitored throughout.
Rituximab: cytokine release syndrome risk. Monitor for PML. Check hepatitis B status.
Maximum 12 four-weekly cycles of lenalidomide. Patient must have had no previous lenalidomide treatment.
National Patient Safety Agency alert NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy.

drugs.notes: 20mg once a day days 1-21 inclusive of 28-day cycle. Available as 20mg, 15mg, 10mg, 7.5mg, 5mg and 2.5mg capsules. Swallow whole with water. Pregnancy prevention programme mandatory.

Cycle 1 only: rituximab 375mg/m² IV on days 1, 8, 15, 22. Intravenous infusion in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Dose rounded to nearest 100mg (up if halfway). Pre-medication: chlorphenamine 10mg IV + hydrocortisone 100mg IV + paracetamol 1000mg oral 30 min prior.

Cycles 2-5: rituximab 1400mg subcutaneous injection over approximately 5 minutes on day 1. Injected into abdominal wall. Cycles 6-12: lenalidomide only (no rituximab). SC pre-medication: non-sedating oral antihistamine + paracetamol 1000mg oral 15-30 min prior.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery. Restart at full dose.
Start at 10mg once a day. Can increase to 15mg after 2 cycles if no response.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: CrCl less than 30 ml/min (not requiring dialysis)
Lenalidomide: CrCl less than 30 ml/min requiring dialysis
dose_modifications.notes: 5mg once a day. On dialysis days, administer following dialysis.
Each SC injection of rituximab should only be given if neutrophil count at least 1x10⁹/L and platelet count at least 50x10⁹/L.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: venous thrombosis or embolism NCI-CTC grade 3 or above
dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment and start anticoagulation. May be reinstated at clinician's discretion.

pre_medications: Cycle 1 rituximab IV pre-medication: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous + Hydrocortisone 100mg intravenous + Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min prior)
Cycles 2-5 rituximab SC pre-medication: non-sedating oral antihistamine (e.g. cetirizine 10mg, loratadine 10mg, fexofenadine 120mg, or acrivastine 8mg) + Paracetamol 1000mg oral (15-30 min prior)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral
Allopurinol 300mg once a day for 7 days cycle 1 only (TLS prophylaxis in patients at risk)
Thromboprophylaxis for patients with additional VTE risk factors
special_warnings: Multiple protocol versions in use - ensure correct protocol for relevant diagnosis (IV/SC version vs IV-only version).
Cycle 1 rituximab given IV (four weekly doses days 1, 8, 15, 22). Cycles 2-5 rituximab given SC (1400mg fixed dose, day 1). Cycles 6-12 lenalidomide monotherapy only.

Subcutaneous rituximab 1400mg fixed dose (not weight-based). Administer over approximately 5 minutes into abdominal wall. Do not inject into areas where skin is red, bruised, tender, hard or has moles/scars.
Pregnancy prevention programme: patient, prescriber and pharmacy must comply. Lenalidomide is teratogenic.
DVT prophylaxis mandatory for patients with additional VTE risk factors. VTE risk assessment prior to starting treatment.
Tumour lysis syndrome: weekly biochemistry monitoring during cycle 1. Adequate hydration required.
Formal medical review at least by end of first 8 weeks of treatment.
Thyroid function tests at baseline and regular intervals. Calcium and magnesium levels monitored throughout.
Rituximab: cytokine release syndrome risk. Monitor for PML. Check hepatitis B status.
National Patient Safety Agency alert NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy.

drugs.notes: 25mg once a day days 1-21 inclusive of 28-day cycle. Available as 25mg, 15mg, 10mg and 5mg tablets. Tablets should be swallowed whole, not chewed. Pregnancy prevention programme mandatory.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery. Restart at full dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: CrCl less than 30 ml/min (not requiring dialysis)
Lenalidomide: CrCl less than 30 ml/min requiring dialysis

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below. Reduce to 15mg, then 10mg, then 5mg on subsequent occurrences. Stop if 5mg not tolerated.
Withhold until resolved to grade 1 or below. Restart cautiously at 15mg. Grade 3 or above: discontinue permanently.

dose_modifications.condition: Lenalidomide: desquamating rash NCI-CTC grade 3 or above, or grade 4 non-desquamating rash
Lenalidomide: venous thrombosis or embolism NCI-CTC grade 3 or above

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral
Allopurinol 300mg once a day cycle 1 only
Thromboprophylaxis in patients with additional VTE risk factors

Pregnancy prevention programme: patient, prescriber and pharmacy must comply. Lenalidomide is teratogenic.
DVT prophylaxis mandatory for patients with additional VTE risk factors. VTE risk assessment prior to starting.
National Patient Safety Agency alert NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed for oral chemotherapy.
It must be made clear to all staff (including community) that this is a short course of oral chemotherapy that must not be continued.
Patients should be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
Dose step-wise reduction ladder: 25mg → 15mg → 10mg → 5mg. Stop if 5mg not tolerated.
Thyroid function tests at baseline and regular intervals. Calcium and magnesium levels monitored throughout.
This protocol may require funding approval.

protocol_name: Lisocabtagene Maraleucel (Lisocel) - Cyclophosphamide 300 - Fludarabine 30 CAR-T Lymphodepletion

drugs.notes: Lymphodepletion chemotherapy. Intravenous bolus over 10 minutes on days -5, -4, -3. Dose banded in accordance with national dose banding table (20mg/ml). Non-vesicant.

Lymphodepletion chemotherapy. Intravenous infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on days -5, -4, -3. Dose banded according to national dose band (25mg/ml). Non-vesicant. Patients treated with fludarabine carry a LIFELONG risk of transfusion-associated GVHD - must receive irradiated blood products for life.

Target dose: 100 x10⁶ CAR+ viable cells (dosing range 44-120 x10⁶). Consists of CD8+ and CD4+ cell components (target 1:1 ratio). Day 0: administered as IV infusion from 2 or more syringes at approximately 0.5ml/minute via a giving set with non-leukodepleting in-line filter primed with sodium chloride 0.9%. Must be administered within 2 hours from start of thaw. CD8+ component must be infused first, followed immediately by CD4+ component. Do not administer via volumetric pump. Administration may be via central or peripheral line. Only available at Wessex Blood and Marrow Transplant Unit. Autologous use only.

dose_modifications.notes: As directed by cell therapy consultant
As directed by cell therapy consultant
No dose modification recommended for hepatic dysfunction
Severe hepatic impairment may decrease activation of cyclophosphamide - consider when selecting dose

dose_modifications.condition: Lisocel infusion: active unresolved serious adverse events or active infections/inflammatory disorders or active GVHD
dose_modifications.notes: Delay Lisocel infusion if patient has unresolved serious adverse events, active infections or inflammatory disorders, or active GVHD.

pre_medications: Chlorphenamine 10mg intravenous (30 min prior to Lisocel on day 0)
Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min prior to Lisocel on day 0)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day oral or intravenous
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral or intravenous
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice a day
Pentamidine 300mg nebuliser during lymphodepletion, then every 28 days until count recovery sufficient for co-trimoxazole
Fluconazole 100mg once a day (or posaconazole 300mg once daily if prolonged neutropenia or previous invasive fungal infection)
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist from first day of lymphodepletion until platelet count >50x10⁹/L
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg (max 800mg) IV 8-hourly if required for CRS (maximum 4 doses - must be prescribed in advance of CAR-T infusion)
Levetiracetam 500mg twice daily oral commencing day 0 until day +28 (seizure prophylaxis, reduce to 250mg twice daily for 2 weeks then stop)
Tumour lysis syndrome prophylaxis per individual patient risk: Allopurinol 300mg oral once a day or Rasburicase 3mg IV once a day
NOTE: Prophylactic systemic corticosteroids are NOT recommended as they may interfere with CAR-T activity
special_warnings: CAR-T THERAPY - LIFE-THREATENING CRS AND ICANS RISK: Nearly all patients experience some degree of CRS including life-threatening and fatal reactions. CRS symptoms: pyrexia, tiredness, cardiac failure, tachycardia, dysrhythmias, dyspnoea, hypoxia, capillary leak, chills, renal impairment, headache, nausea. Temperature, BP and O2 saturation monitored 4-hourly after Lisocel on day 0 then twice daily.
CAR-T cell therapy: single administration procedure. cycle_length_days is not applicable — this is a one-time infusion following lymphodepletion conditioning.

ICANS (immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome): symptoms include seizures, somnolence, headaches, confusion, agitation, speech disorders, tremor, encephalopathy, ataxia, memory impairment, hallucinations, delirium. ICE score assessed twice daily.

LIFELONG IRRADIATED BLOOD PRODUCTS: Fludarabine causes lifelong risk of transfusion-associated GVHD. ALL blood products must be irradiated for life. Local blood transfusion department must be notified immediately.
Patient must be issued with a Lisocel treatment alert card to carry at all times (CRS/ICANS can occur weeks after infusion).

Lymphodepleting regimen must only be started after availability of Lisocel is confirmed. A minimum period must elapse between last lymphodepletion dose and CAR-T infusion (see patient CAR-T schedule).
CAR-T administration must not occur out of core hours or over the weekend.
Lisocel cells are genetically modified human blood cells - GMO spill-kit must accompany Lisocel. Strict PPE and waste disposal procedures.
Indications: DLBCL/PMBCL/FL3B relapsed within 12 months of or refractory to first-line chemoimmunotherapy; OR relapsed/refractory DLBCL/PMBCL/FL3B after 2+ lines of systemic therapy.
Not recommended for patients with active GVHD or who have undergone allogeneic stem cell transplant with active acute/chronic GVHD.
Echocardiogram and baseline lung function must be performed prior to initiating lymphodepleting chemotherapy.
HBV, HCV and HIV screening must be performed before collection of cells for Lisocel manufacture.
Regimen summary header shows CYCLOPHOSPHAMIDE (500) in the Aria regimen summary page title but the actual dose is 300mg/m² as stated throughout the protocol.
Any suspected adverse reaction to CAR-T cell infusion should be reported via MHRA Yellow Card scheme.
